implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-003: Unsubscribe on every exit path
AC-004: None/unavailable client is no-op + structured warning
AC-005: Per-(build_id, subcommand) subject scoping; parallel invocations independent
AC-006: Per-instance sinks isolate concurrent builds
AC-007: Malformed payloads dropped with structured warning
AC-008: Unit tests with fake NATS client cover all listed scenarios
AC-009: ruff check + ruff format both clean

completion_promises.evidence: Implemented `subscribe_progress` as an `@asynccontextmanager` in `src/forge/adapters/guardkit/progress_subscriber.py`. Verified via `TestModuleSurface::test_subscribe_progress_is_async_context_manager` (object exposes `__aenter__`/`__aexit__`) and `test_module_path` (canonical module path).

completion_promises.criterion_text: ProgressSink retains the most recent event per (build_id, subcommand) pair; old events are evicted under back-pressure (Scenario 'Progress events emitted faster than Forge consumes them are still observable for live status')
completion_promises.evidence: ProgressSink stores per-key `collections.deque(maxlen=N)` so append() drops the oldest entry on full — exactly the 'most recent observable' guarantee. Verified by `TestProgressSinkRetention::test_back_pressure_drops_oldest` (5 events into a 3-slot sink — last 3 retained, oldest 2 evicted) plus `test_latest_returns_most_recent`.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Unsubscribe runs on context-manager exit, including the exception path
completion_promises.evidence: The async context manager wraps `yield` in a `try/finally` and awaits `sub.unsubscribe()` in the finally block. A second try/except inside the finally swallows unsubscribe failures so cleanup never propagates. Verified by `TestUnsubscribeOnExit::test_unsubscribes_on_normal_exit`, `test_unsubscribes_on_exception_exit` (RuntimeError raised inside the body), and `test_unsubscribe_failure_does_not_propagate`.

completion_promises.criterion_text: If the NATS client is None or unavailable, subscribe_progress yields a no-op subscription that records a single progress_stream_unavailable warning to the sink and the surrounding call still proceeds (Scenario 'The authoritative result still returns when progress streaming is unavailable')
completion_promises.evidence: Two no-op paths implemented: (a) `nats_client is None` records one `PROGRESS_STREAM_UNAVAILABLE` warning, logs, and yields; (b) `subscribe()` raising likewise records the warning and yields. Verified by `TestUnavailableClient::test_none_client_is_noop_and_warns` (asserts `inside is True`, exactly one warning with code `progress_stream_unavailable`) and `test_subscribe_failure_yields_noop` (uses `BrokenNATSClient` raising `ConnectionError`).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Each subscription is scoped to one pipeline.stage-complete.{build_id}.{subcommand} subject — two parallel invocations within the same build receive independent event streams (Scenario 'Parallel GuardKit invocations in the same build do not corrupt each other's results')
completion_promises.evidence: `subject_for(build_id, subcommand)` builds the exact subject; `subscribe_progress` only subscribes to that one subject. Verified by `TestSubjectScoping::test_subscribes_to_correct_subject` (asserts the subscribe topic literal) and `test_parallel_subscriptions_are_independent` (two `async with` blocks for the same build_id but distinct subcommands; each sink slot receives only its own events).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Two concurrent builds against the same repo get isolated sinks (no shared state, ASSUM-007)
completion_promises.evidence: ProgressSink owns no class- or module-level state — each instance has its own `_buffers` and `_warnings`. Verified by `TestParallelBuilds::test_two_builds_have_isolated_sinks`: two distinct sinks for two build_ids; events delivered on each subject only land in the corresponding sink, and querying the cross-build slot returns None.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Invalid payloads (malformed JSON, missing fields) are dropped with a structured warning, never raised
completion_promises.evidence: The inbound `_on_envelope` callback wraps `GuardKitProgressEvent.model_validate(envelope.payload)` in a try/except catching `ValidationError`, `ValueError`, `TypeError`; on failure it records a `PROGRESS_PAYLOAD_INVALID` warning and returns (never raises). Verified by `TestMalformedPayloads::test_missing_required_field_dropped`, `test_wrong_payload_type_dropped`, and `test_valid_event_after_malformed_still_recorded` (subscription continues consuming after a bad payload).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Unit tests with a fake NATS client: ordered delivery, back-pressure eviction, malformed payload, unavailable client, parallel subscriptions
completion_promises.evidence: Hand-rolled `FakeNATSClient` and `BrokenNATSClient` (no nats-py imports). Coverage: ordered delivery (`test_ordered_delivery_within_one_subscription`), back-pressure (`test_back_pressure_drops_oldest`), malformed payload (3 cases under TestMalformedPayloads), unavailable client (`test_none_client_is_noop_and_warns`, `test_subscribe_failure_yields_noop`), parallel subscriptions (`test_parallel_subscriptions_are_independent`, `test_two_builds_have_isolated_sinks`). 22 tests, all passing.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: `ruff check` reports 'All checks passed!' on both files. `ruff format --check` reports '2 files already formatted' after applying the project's default ruff format. No new linter rules added.
